results.prompt: Build the first runnable preview of a next-generation full-stack monorepo starter. Do not ask follow-up questions. The preview must visibly include a Next.js app-router/RSC web app, shared tRPC API, edge-ready database layer using Kysely plus Prisma schema management, PlanetScale-style database status, Clerk-style auth, shadcn-style UI blocks, Expo mobile workspace notes, and a monorepo health panel.

dom.title: Next-Gen Monorepo Console
dom.text:
FULL-STACK APP-ROUTER STARTER

Next-Gen Monorepo Console

A runnable preview for a Next.js app-router/RSC monorepo with shared tRPC API, Kysely plus Prisma schema management, PlanetScale edge database status, Clerk auth, shadcn-style UI blocks, and Expo workspace notes.

NEXT.JS
App Router + RSC
API
shared tRPC
DATABASE
Kysely + Prisma
AUTH
Clerk session
MONOREPO
Workspace map

The starter separates apps/web, apps/expo, packages/api, packages/db, and packages/ui so the monorepo shape is obvious.

NEXT.JS
App-router/RSC web app

The web lane highlights server components, route groups, loading states, and typed server actions.

TRPC
Shared API contract

A shared tRPC router powers web and Expo clients without duplicating request types.

KYSELY
Edge-ready query layer

Kysely handles type-safe queries while Prisma owns schema management and migrations.

PLANETSCALE
Database health

A PlanetScale-style status card shows edge driver readiness, schema sync, and query latency.

CLERK
Auth and UI blocks

Clerk auth, shadcn-style cards, and Expo mobile workspace notes make the starter feel complete.

Action loop
Open monorepo health
Inspect tRPC API
Check PlanetScale status
Preview Expo notes

contents..github/workflows/ci.yml:
name: GitHub Actions CI
on: [push, pull_request]
jobs:
  test:
    runs-on: ubuntu-latest
    steps:
      - uses: actions/checkout@v4
      - uses: pnpm/action-setup@v4
      - uses: actions/setup-node@v4
        with:
          node-version: 22
          cache: pnpm
      - run: pnpm install --frozen-lockfile
      - run: pnpm test

results.prompt: Create an unofficial Python SDK package for a file-upload service. It must include pyproject metadata, an async UTApi client with list_files and delete_file, typed models, a FastAPI route-handler adapter, CORS example, environment variable usage, and a quickstart script.
results.response:
Unofficial Python SDK package with async UTApi client, typed models, FastAPI adapter, CORS example, UPLOADTHING_SECRET usage, and quickstart.

```toml title="pyproject.toml"
[project]
name = "uploadthing-py"
version = "0.1.0"
description = "Unofficial async Python SDK for a file-upload service"
requires-python = ">=3.11"
dependencies = ["httpx>=0.27", "pydantic>=2", "fastapi>=0.111"]

[tool.pytest.ini_options]
asyncio_mode = "auto"
```

```py title="uploadthing_py/__init__.py"
from .client import UTApi
from .fastapi import create_route_handler

__all__ = ["UTApi", "create_route_handler"]
```

```py title="uploadthing_py/client.py"
from dataclasses import dataclass
import httpx

@dataclass(frozen=True)
class UploadFile:
    key: str
    name: str
    size: int

class UTApi:
    def __init__(self, secret: str, base_url: str = "https://api.uploadthing.com"):
        self.secret = secret
        self.base_url = base_url.rstrip("/")

    async def list_files(self) -> list[UploadFile]:
        async with httpx.AsyncClient() as client:
            res = await client.get(f"{self.base_url}/v6/listFiles", headers={"x-uploadthing-api-key": self.secret})
            res.raise_for_status()
            return [UploadFile(**item) for item in res.json().get("files", [])]

    async def delete_file(self, key: str) -> dict:
        async with httpx.AsyncClient() as client:
            res = await client.post(f"{self.base_url}/v6/deleteFiles", json={"fileKeys": [key]}, headers={"x-uploadthing-api-key": self.secret})
            res.raise_for_status()
            return res.json()
```

```py title="uploadthing_py/fastapi.py"
import os
from fastapi import APIRouter, FastAPI, Request, Response
from fastapi.middleware.cors import CORSMiddleware
from .client import UTApi

def create_route_handler(secret: str | None = None) -> APIRouter:
    router = APIRouter()
    utapi = UTApi(secret or os.environ["UPLOADTHING_SECRET"])

    @router.get("/files")
    async def list_files():
        return await utapi.list_files()

    @router.delete("/files/{key}")
    async def delete_file(key: str):
        return await utapi.delete_file(key)

    return router

def install_cors(app: FastAPI) -> None:
    app.add_middleware(CORSMiddleware, allow_origins=["*"], allow_methods=["*"], allow_headers=["*"])
```

```py title="examples/fastapi_app.py"
from fastapi import FastAPI
from uploadthing_py.fastapi import create_route_handler, install_cors

app = FastAPI()
install_cors(app)
app.include_router(create_route_handler(), prefix="/api/uploadthing")
```

```py title="examples/quickstart.py"
import asyncio, os
from uploadthing_py import UTApi

async def main():
    api = UTApi(os.environ["UPLOADTHING_SECRET"])
    files = await api.list_files()
    print("List files:", files)
    if files:
        print("Delete file:", await api.delete_file(files[0].key))

if __name__ == "__main__":
    asyncio.run(main())
```

results.prompt: Build a runnable demo for a theme kit that makes TanStack Start-style apps easy to theme. The preview must include a ThemeProvider concept, light/dark/system toggle, SSR-safe initial script explanation, localStorage persistence, CSS variables, route cards, and install snippets for npm, JSR, and Deno.

dom.title: TanStack Theme Kit
dom.text:
THEMEPROVIDER DEMO

TanStack Theme Kit

A runnable demo for TanStack Start-style theming with ThemeProvider, light/dark/system modes, SSR-safe initial script guidance, localStorage persistence, CSS variables, and install snippets for npm, JSR, and Deno.

THEMEPROVIDER
React context
MODES
light / dark / system
PERSISTENCE
localStorage
PACKAGES
npm / JSR / Deno
TANSTACK
Start route cards

Route cards show how a TanStack Start app can inherit the same theme tokens across pages.

THEMEPROVIDER
Provider boundary

The provider owns theme state, system preference resolution, and CSS variable application.

SSR-SAFE INITIAL SCRIPT
No flash on first paint

The initial script explains how to set the class before hydration so light, dark, and system modes stay stable.

LOCALSTORAGE
Remember preference

Selected themes persist locally and can be reset to system when the user wants automatic mode.

CSS VARIABLES
Token surface

Semantic variables for background, foreground, accent, and border make app-wide theming predictable.

JSR / DENO
Install snippets

The preview includes npm, JSR, and Deno commands so non-Node runtimes are represented.

Action loop
Toggle light
Toggle dark
Use system
Copy JSR install

contents.README.md:
# Tailwind Canonical Classes Autofix

A VS Code extension that auto-fixes Tailwind CSS canonical class suggestions on save by reading Tailwind CSS IntelliSense diagnostics shaped like `The class ... can be written as ...`.

## Setting

- `tailwindCanonicalClasses.fixOnSave`: enable or disable applying canonical class fixes on save.

results.prompt: Build a runnable kanban board preview inspired by bleeding-edge full-stack TypeScript. It must visibly include Trellix-style columns, draggable-looking task cards, tRPC procedure/status panel, Auth.js session area, Turso/libSQL database status, realtime activity, and setup reminders for TURSO_URL, TURSO_ACCESS_TOKEN, and AUTH_SECRET.

dom.title: Trellix tRPC Board
dom.text:
BLEEDING-EDGE TYPESCRIPT KANBAN

Trellix tRPC Board

A runnable Trellix-style kanban board preview with draggable-looking task cards, tRPC procedure status, Auth.js session state, Turso/libSQL database health, realtime activity, and setup reminders for TURSO_URL, TURSO_ACCESS_TOKEN, and AUTH_SECRET.

TRELLIX
Kanban preview
TRPC
Procedures ready
AUTH.JS
Session active
TURSO
libSQL healthy
KANBAN
Backlog column

Seeded cards look draggable and show priority, assignee, and due date metadata.

TRPC
Procedure status panel

Board queries and mutation names are listed so the API surface is visible.

AUTH.JS
Session area

Mock sign-in state shows how board ownership and member access will be enforced.

TURSO
Database status

Turso/libSQL connection cards call out TURSO_URL and TURSO_ACCESS_TOKEN setup.

AUTH_SECRET
Environment checklist

AUTH_SECRET, GitHub OAuth, and database push reminders sit next to the preview.

REALTIME
Activity feed

Realtime activity explains card moves, comments, and sync events as the board changes.

Action loop
Move kanban card
Call tRPC mutation
Check Turso status
Review AUTH_SECRET
